stories.headline: Iran Says Hormuz to Open During Lebanon Truce in Boost for Peace
stories.summary: Iran said it would open the Strait of Hormuz for the duration of a 10-day ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah in Lebanon, increasing the prospect of an agreement to end the wider war in the Middle East. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: First cruise ship transits the Strait of Hormuz since Iran war began
stories.summary: Iran and the US said the strategic waterway, which has been effectively closed since the beginning of the war, will be fully open to commercial traffic for the remainder of the ceasefire

stories.headline: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait 'open'
stories.summary: Brent crude sinks by a tenth after Iran says the key waterway is open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.

stories.headline: Oil slumps as US and Iran declare Strait of Hormuz open to shipping
stories.summary: Trump suggests warring sides are closer to ending a war that has sent shockwaves through markets and economies

stories.headline: Oil Tankers Make Dash Toward Hormuz as Iran Says Strait Is Open
stories.summary: At least eight oil tankers raced toward the Strait of Hormuz in the hours after Iran’s foreign minister said the vital waterway was fully open to shipping.

stories.headline: Shipowners and Oil Traders Wary as Iran Says Hormuz Fully Open
stories.summary: Tanker owners and oil traders responded warily after Iran said on Friday that the Strait of Hormuz is open to all ships — a statement that, if true, will free up millions of barrels of crude oil and fuel stuck inside the Persian Gulf.

stories.headline: Stock Rally, Oil Drops on Hormuz Hope | Open Interest 4/17/2026
stories.summary: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest." Oil slides again as markets react to signals the Strait of Hormuz is open. Netflix disappoints investors as Reed Hastings steps down after nearly three decades. And Anthropic’s CEO heads to the White House as AI tensions cool. Oklo CEO Jake DeWitte explains why nuclear power could fuel the future of space. Brian Kelly reveals why airfare keeps cl

stories.headline: Macron demands assurances after Iran opens Strait of Hormuz
stories.summary: French President Emmanuel Macron said he welcomed the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the Lebanon ceasefire.

stories.headline: Iran foreign minister says Strait of Hormuz ‘completely open’
stories.summary: Iran says 'passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz' open during the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.

stories.headline: Oil prices drop over 10% after Iran declares the Strait of Hormuz 'completely open'
stories.summary: Oil prices have dropped over 10% in less than two hours after the Minister for Foreign Affairs of Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz "completely open" for the remainder of the current ceasefire with the US.

stories.headline: Israeli attacks kill several over two days in Gaza despite ‘ceasefire’
stories.summary: As Gaza violence continues, activist says Israeli settlers torch vehicles, attack homes in occupied West Bank.

stories.headline: Trump Says Iran Is Removing Mines From Hormuz Strait
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says Iran is removing sea mines from the Strait of Hormuz. He also says NATO is now offering to help secure the waterway. Bloomberg's Alaric Nightingale reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: EU prepares for jet fuel sharing as supplies dwindle
stories.summary: Energy commissioner Dan Jørgensen says Europe moving towards supply crisis “very rapidly”

stories.headline: Oil flows from Russia via Druzhba pipeline to Hungary could resume next week, Magyar says
stories.summary: The pipeline, which delivers Russian oil to Hungary via Ukraine, has been a source of contention between Budapest and Kyiv after flows were stopped when it sustained damage in a drone strike.

stories.headline: ‘Waiting without answers’: Gaza mother’s fear for her three imprisoned sons
stories.summary: As Palestinians mark Prisoners' Day, Inaam al-Dahdouh waits for news of her three sons, taken by Israel two years ago.

stories.headline: Australia's most decorated living soldier released on bail over war crime charges
stories.summary: Lawyers argued that Ben Roberts-Smith wouldn't be able to defend himself properly from prison.

stories.headline: Harry and Meghan meet Bondi shooting survivors
stories.summary: On the fourth day of their Australian visit, the royal couple pay tribute to the 15 people killed in the Bondi shooting.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran peace talks will ‘probably’ resume this weekend
stories.summary: US stocks head for their best month in six years on hopes of an end to the war

stories.headline: Trump Says Iran Agrees to Unlimited Suspension of Nuclear Program
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said in a phone interview on Friday that Iran agreed to suspend its nuclear program indefinitely and will not receive any frozen funds from the US in exchange for the move. Tyler Kendall reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Ben Roberts-Smith released from prison on bail after being charged with five counts of war crime murder
stories.summary: Former SAS corporal granted bail ahead of potential trial on charges relating to alleged killing of civilians in AfghanistanFollow our Australia news live blog for latest updatesGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastBen Roberts-Smith has been granted bail under strict conditions while he awaits a potential trial on alleged war crimes.The Victoria Cross recipient, once Australia’s most lionised soldier, faces five charges of w

stories.headline: Macron and Starmer host allies for summit on Hormuz maritime security
stories.summary: US not part of France- and UK-led talks on a post-war Hormuz maritime force, but Europe's role remains limited.

stories.headline: EU downplays jet fuel shortage risks despite IEA warning
stories.summary: EU officials and the International Energy Agency have contrasting narratives over jet fuel supply. While the European commission minimises the risk, it acknowledges that the market remains “tight” and says the EU is prepared to respond appropriately.

stories.headline: Irish fugitive and suspected crime boss Daniel Kinahan arrested in Dubai
stories.summary: Kinahan, in his 40s, was arrested in Dubai on foot of an arrest warrant issued by the Irish courts.

stories.headline: Avengers reassemble and Top Gun flies back - Hollywood studios preview their new movies
stories.summary: Some of the most hotly anticipated new films of the next couple of years are previewed at CinemaCon.

stories.headline: Starmer blames ‘unforgivable’ Foreign Office for vetting fiasco
stories.summary: PM facing calls to resign after Peter Mandelson revealed to have failed security officials’ checks

stories.headline: Hedge Funds’ Treasury Bets Risk Market Shock, Apollo’s Slok Warns
stories.summary: A buildup of leveraged hedge fund bets in Treasuries has left investors exposed to abrupt position shifts that could amplify stress across global bond markets, according to Apollo Global Management Chief Economist Torsten Slok.

stories.headline: Warsh Hearing Will Test How Far He’ll Push Balance Sheet Reform
stories.summary: Kevin Warsh will get his first major public platform as Federal Reserve Chair nominee next week to address his longstanding concerns over the central bank’s outsized market footprint.

stories.headline: Fed's Daly Says Economy Solid, Consumers Still Spending
stories.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco President Mary Daly says the war in Iran and the resulting oil price shock have made the path of Fed policy uncertain during remarks at UC Berkeley. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Supreme Court Sides With Oil Companies in Louisiana Coastal Lawsuits
stories.summary: The companies had asked the justices to clear the way to move environmental lawsuits out of state courts, to friendlier federal venues.

stories.headline: Acting ICE Director Says He Plans to Resign in May
stories.summary: Todd Lyons said he would leave to spend more time with his family. He has spoken about a surge in threats against ICE officers, saying that he knew the reality firsthand.

stories.headline: Editor's Choice: Energy crisis spreads across Asia, but investors stay bullish
What to know about OpenAI's new model for life sciences research GPT-Rosalind
stories.summary: The GPT-Rosalind model is designed to accelerate biological research and drug discovery.

stories.headline: Italy: 25 hostages freed as Naples bank gang vanishes underground
stories.summary: A group of three masked robbers held 25 people hostage during a bank heist in Naples on Thursday 16 April 2026 before escaping through a tunnel connected to the city’s sewer network, authorities said.

stories.headline: Nearly 900 Rohingya refugees dead and missing in shipwrecks in 2025, UNHCR says
stories.summary: The Rohingya mainly leave from huge camps in Bangladesh, where more than 1 million refugees forced to flee across the border from Myanmar's Rakhine state live in squalid conditions.

stories.headline: Thousands celebrate open-air Mass with Pope Leo in Cameroon - in pictures
stories.summary: Pope Leo XIV is on his third day in Cameroon before he heads to Angola on Saturday.

stories.headline: Finance ministers and top bankers raise serious concerns about Mythos AI model
stories.summary: Experts say Mythos potentially has an unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cyber-security weaknesses.

stories.headline: What next for South African opposition firebrand Malema after his five-year prison sentence?
stories.summary: The sentence raises huge questions marks over the political future of one of South Africa's most controversial politicians.

stories.headline: Artemis commander tells BBC about 'powerful' moment crater named after his late wife
stories.summary: Reid Wiseman’s two daughters were in Nasa’s mission control room for the naming of the “Carroll” crater in honour of the commander’s late wife.

stories.headline: Chinese carmaker patents voice-controlled 'in-vehicle toilet'
stories.summary: Seres' plans show how stiff competition in the EV space is putting pressure on carmakers to innovate.

stories.headline: Major refinery fire won't lead to fuel rationing, Australian PM says
stories.summary: A 13-hour blaze at one of Australia's two oil refineries could impact petrol prices and supply.

stories.headline: Lloyd’s of London to end flagship diversity event
stories.summary: Insurance marketplace has for years faced claims of sexual harassment and inappropriate workplace behaviour

stories.headline: Why Coutts is making an unexpected play for video games
stories.summary: The over 300-year-old lender is touting its status as a major banker to the sector

stories.headline: Brazil Treasury Sees Room for More Foreign-Exchange-Linked Debt
stories.summary: Brazil’s government sees room to grow its share of foreign-exchange-linked debt even further following this year’s planned expansion, Treasury Secretary Daniel Leal said in an interview.

stories.headline: Short Seller Targets AQR Backer Over Tax-Loss Harvesting
stories.summary: A short seller is taking aim at the more than $1 trillion that’s invested in strategies that reduce taxes for the rich — putting one of the US’ most staid money managers in its crosshairs.

stories.headline: Medical Device Maker Alamar Bio Jumps 33% After Upsized US IPO
stories.summary: Alamar Biosciences Inc. shares climbed 33% on Friday after the medical device maker raised $191 million in an upsized US initial public offering that priced at the top of a marketed range.

stories.headline: US Treasuries Rally as Falling Oil Boosts Rate-Cut Bets
stories.summary: Treasuries jumped, sending yields to their lowest levels in a month, as easing Middle East tensions drove oil lower and pushed traders to price roughly even odds of a Federal Reserve rate cut this year.

stories.headline: House Votes to Extend Expiring FISA Surveillance Law for 10 Days
stories.summary: The Senate approved a stopgap measure that passed the House early Friday. Libertarian-leaning House Republicans had balked at a long-term extension.

stories.headline: Panel Advances Trump’s Triumphal Arch, Even as Key Member Suggests Changes
stories.summary: The Commission of Fine Arts, a Trump-aligned advisory body, granted preliminary approval. But its vice chairman suggested losing statues atop the structure and other revisions before a final vote.

stories.headline: Ben Roberts-Smith’s comrades say he ordered them to execute unarmed civilians, court documents show

stories.headline: Bulgaria elections: Who’s running and what’s at stake?
stories.summary: Bulgaria has held seven national elections in the past four years because of political and social divisions.

stories.headline: Japan power companies tap home batteries to help cut electricity bills
Kweichow Moutai sees 1st fall in revenue, profit amid corruption crusade
Philippine inequalities deepen as jeepneys languish and oil companies gain
10-day ceasefire in Lebanon begins as Israel agrees to U.S.-backed deal
stories.summary: President Donald Trump announced the agreement, which went into force Thursday evening, as Pakistani mediators worked to extend a U.S.-Iran ceasefire and arrange new talks.

stories.headline: Trump says Iran agrees to hand over ‘nuclear dust’
stories.summary: Iran has not confirmed Trump’s claim. Giving up its highly enriched uranium would be a major step toward an agreement.

stories.headline: Naples bank robbers hold 25 people hostage then vanish through tunnel
stories.summary: The armed men reportedly evaded capture by escaping through the city's sewer system.

stories.headline: Pope criticises 'tyrants' who spend billions on wars after Trump spat
stories.summary: The comments follow a high-profile spat with US President Donald Trump, who called the Pope weak on crime.

stories.headline: Turkish police detain 162 people over online posts about school shootings
stories.summary: At least 16 people were injured in a shooting at a high school on Tuesday, before another nine were killed in a separate school shooting on Wednesday.

stories.headline: Europe has 'maybe six weeks of jet fuel left', energy boss warns
stories.summary: Flights could soon be cancelled if supplies from the Gulf remain blocked, says the International Energy Agency.

stories.headline: Russia launches deadliest aerial attack in months, killing 18 in Ukraine
stories.summary: A Ukrainian drone attack killed two people in Russia, Moscow says, after an Orthodox Easter truce.

stories.headline: Japan pledges $10bn to help Asian countries deal with oil crisis
stories.summary: The aid is roughly equivalent to a year's worth of crude oil imports by Asean countries.

stories.headline: House Votes to Preserve Deportation Protections for Haitians, Rebuking Trump
stories.summary: The action was largely symbolic since the president would be all but certain to veto the bill, but the bipartisan vote reflected resistance within his own ranks on his signature issue.

stories.headline: House votes to restore protections for Haitians, defying Trump
stories.summary: Ten House Republicans joined Democrats to oppose President Donald Trump on his immigration policy Thursday, voting to restore temporary protections for Haitians living in the United States.

stories.headline: How South Korea plans to use the Iran crisis to spur a renewables revolution
stories.summary: Energy crisis unfolding in Middle East has added political urgency, and more funding, to transform South Korea’s solar industryIn Guyang-ri, a farming village of 70 households about 90 minutes south-east of Seoul, people gather for communal free lunches six days a week. stories.headline: UK seeks closer EU ties in volatile times - but at what cost?
stories.summary: The UK is adopting a "ruthlessly pragmatic" approach to becoming closer to its European neighbours, the UK's EU minister tells the BBC.

stories.headline: 'How does one survive?': Factory protests expose strain in India's industrial system
stories.summary: Workers in some north Indian cities have been protesting, demanding better pay and working conditions.

stories.headline: 'Unprecedented' fire at Australian oil refinery to affect nation's petrol supplies
stories.summary: The fire has deepened fears over the nation's petrol supplies amid a global crunch.

stories.headline: Trump to Pick Ousted FEMA Head to Lead Agency Again
stories.summary: Cameron Hamilton is a former Navy SEAL who ran unsuccessfully for Congress.

stories.headline: Trump hints Iran talks could resume this week as US port blockade continues
stories.summary: The United Nations' secretary general said it was "highly probable" that the negotiations would restart.

stories.headline: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.summary: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.headline: Trump says China has agreed not to send weapons to Iran
stories.summary: The president made the claim, which Beijing has yet to confirm, amid anger in China that its vessels could be caught up in a U.S. blockade targeting Iranian ports.

stories.headline: Magyar calls for fast handover of power in talks with Hungary's president
stories.summary: Péter Magyar, the man who ended Viktor Orbán's 16 years of continuous rule, wants the new parliament to convene in early May.

stories.headline: Why and how is US blockading Iranian ports in Strait of Hormuz?
stories.summary: Donald Trump says that the US is blockading the Strait of Hormuz. What does this mean in practice?

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said migrants helped "build the rich, open and diverse Spain that we are and to which we aspire".

stories.headline: Bongbong Marcos fights ill-health rumours with star jumps
stories.summary: The Philippine President challenged anyone questioning his fitness to join him in the gym.

stories.headline: How Trump’s Jesus-like image and feud with the Pope are sparking backlash
stories.summary: BBC's Sarah Smith looks at how the president's most ardent supporters are reacting to his latest social media posts.

stories.headline: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
stories.summary: Technical and logistical problems mean Peruvians choosing a new president and members of Congress continue to vote on Monday.
